channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Uber's lobbying influences Halifax council on ride-hailing oversight
item.description: Newly released documents show that Halifax Mayor Andy Fillmore met with an Uber lobbyist just a day before persuading the city council to delay a vote on stricter regulations for ride-hailing drivers....

item.title: President Ramaphosa awards National Orders to South Africans and international allies, highlighting contributions in arts, science and community service
item.description: On 19 May 2026, President Cyril Ramaphosa conferred the National Orders to recognise South Africans and international allies who have made significant contributions to arts, culture, science, business...

item.title: How to Handle Stress While Searching for a Job
item.description: Searching for a job fit can be stressful, and many people forget that it affects not just your career but also your mind and emotions. Long periods without responses, slow feedback, and repeated near-...

item.title: Bushy Maape, former North West premier and liberation veteran, passes away aged 69
item.description: Former North West premier Bushy Maape passed away in Johannesburg on Saturday, 17 May 2026, at 2:49 pm after a short illness, surrounded by his children and family. The passing was confirmed by the cu...

item.title: Key Czechia Players Bafana Bafana Will Face in World Cup 2026
item.description: Czechia secured their place in the 2026 FIFA World Cup after a dramatic penalty shootout victory over Denmark in the playoff final, following a 1-1 draw after extra time. This marks their first World ...

item.title: Sen. Van Hollen confronts Attorney General Blanche over DOJ anti-weaponization fund and pardoned offender case
item.description: During a tense Senate Appropriations Committee hearing, Attorney General Todd Blanche faced strong questioning from Senator Chris Van Hollen (D-MD) regarding the Justice Department's anti-weaponizatio...

item.title: Pakistan prolongs airspace restrictions on Indian aircraft until June 23
item.description: ISLAMABAD – The Pakistan Airports Authority (PAA) has officially extended restrictions on all Indian-registered and Indian-operated aircraft until June 23, as per a fresh Notice to Airmen (Notam) issu...

item.title: Bangladesh close to historic Test series win against Pakistan after Taijul’s four-wicket haul
item.description: Bangladesh are just three wickets away from achieving a historic Test series win against Pakistan at home, thanks to an impressive bowling performance by spinner Taijul Islam, who took 4 wickets for 1...

item.title: A Retrospective Look at Black Sabbath's 1970s Era
item.description: Black Sabbath, formed in Birmingham, England in 1968, became one of the pioneering forces of heavy metal. The original lineup consisted of guitarist Tony Iommi, drummer Bill Ward, bassist Geezer Butle...

item.title: Norwegian Journalist Helle Lyng Faces Online Backlash After Questioning PM Modi on Human Rights
item.description: During Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s visit to Norway, Norwegian journalist Helle Lyng, representing Oslo-based Dagsavisen, faced a wave of online abuse for attempting to ask a question on human right...

item.title: Tulfo Clarifies Minority Senators Did Not Threaten Leadership Change
item.description: Senator Raffy Tulfo has clarified that allegations about the minority bloc threatening senators to shift allegiance and unseat the current Senate leadership are false. During a press briefing, Tulfo e...

item.title: Meghan Markle shares private wedding photos on eighth anniversary, highlighting close friends
item.description: Meghan Markle commemorated her eighth wedding anniversary with Prince Harry by sharing previously unseen photographs from their wedding day on Instagram. The posts included moments from their first da...

item.title: Miami’s Hard Rock Stadium Transforms from F1 to World Cup Ready Venue
item.description: Miami’s Hard Rock Stadium, with a seating capacity of 65,000 in Miami Gardens, is undergoing a rapid transformation from hosting Formula One to preparing for the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The stadium, home...

item.title: Domestic Talents Leaving NPFL Due to Poor Pay and Facilities
item.description: Former Super Eagles captain and coach, Sunday Oliseh, don yan say di best Nigerian domestic football players no dey for NPFL because dem dey waka go foreign for better money. According to am, di poor ...

item.title: Maya Hawke Reflects on Career Fears Following the End of Stranger Things
item.description: Maya Hawke, who portrayed Robin Buckley in Netflix’s hit series Stranger Things from seasons three to five, has spoken candidly about her struggles with depression following the show's conclusion in D...

item.title: Security Tightens as Fuel Protests Cause Transport Disruptions Across Kenya
item.description: On Tuesday, Kenya experienced a gradual return of calm in several towns as police and other security agencies stepped up patrols to contain unrest caused by the ongoing fuel protests. The demonstratio...

item.title: Another case of meningitis confirmed at Reading junior school
item.description: Reports indicate that a fourth pupil at a junior school in Reading has been diagnosed with meningitis, marking a concerning outbreak at the school. Authorities have confirmed the case, and health offi...

item.title: A curated selection of political cartoons published on May 20, 2026
item.description: This piece from The Sydney Morning Herald presents a curated gallery of political cartoons published around May 20, 2026. Rather than a traditional written article, it compiles a series of visual comm...

item.title: U.S. imposes countervailing duties on Canadian mushrooms over disputed subsidies
item.description: The United States has imposed countervailing duties on fresh mushrooms imported from Canada following a preliminary investigation by the U.S. Department of Commerce. Most Canadian mushroom producers w...

item.title: Challenges Faced by Ebola Survivors in Rebuilding Their Lives in Sierra Leone and Liberia
item.description: During the Ebola outbreak from 2014 to 2016, many families for Liberia and Sierra Leone suffered heavy losses. One story na Musu from Monrovia, wey lose six family members to the virus but survive wit...
